summary: External-review improvement cycle addressing berendt's review #3 (3 INFO findings). The single code change in keystone_controller.go:615-632 correctly implements I-001: the owner-ref Get branch now logs at V(1) with structured fields (secret, ownerRef, error) when the error is non-NotFound before falling through to enqueue, restoring operational signal symmetry with the indexed-List branch at line 597. I-002 (1s Consistently window flakiness risk) was captured as a follow-up pattern per the reviewer's ASK classification. I-003 (hardcoded "Keystone" Kind literal) was optional and correctly deferred. Pattern files are updated with both lessons. go vet passes, mapper tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_* pass after change)
Edge cases are covered (NotFound still continues, non-NotFound still enqueues)
No new test needed — logging-only change, behavior unchanged

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (structured logging via log.FromContext)
Feature ID referenced in comment (CC-0087 review #3)
Log fields consistent with sibling branch (secret + ownerRef + error)

security_checklist.item: No new user input, no new external calls
Error value logged safely (no secret data in error field)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — only the intended I-001 fix, no scope creep

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected and logged at the boundary where they occur

defensive_checklist.item: NotFound and non-NotFound branches explicit and distinct

suggested_improvements: I-003 (hardcoded "Keystone" Kind literal) remains at keystone_controller.go:606. Reviewer marked this as optional/ASK. Consider introducing keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneKind constant alongside GroupVersion in a future cleanup if the convention is adopted repo-wide.

I-002 follow-up: if CI flakes materialize in TestIntegration_UnrelatedSecretDoesNotTriggerReconcile, switch the Consistently assertion to a reconcile-counter metric delta that is independent of requeue cadence.
next_steps: Merge this improvement cycle. Both deferred items (I-002, I-003) are tracked in review_patterns and can be addressed opportunistically.
